title: hieracium adunans subsp. coronarium
intro: Hieracium adunans subsp. coronarium, commonly known as the Hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Hawkweed has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Hawkweed include its potential use in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues, although further research is needed to confirm these claims. Hawkweed can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
